title: Crystal Curriculum — TF-IDF curriculum learning by Bee Bytez
body:
## Crystal Curriculum Entry

**Key idea:** TF-IDF-based curriculum learning via the Crystallizer module. Instead of training on random FineWeb sequences, we score candidate batches by information density (token entropy via unigram statistics) and oversample the densest ones.

### Changes from baseline
- **Crystallizer**: lightweight TF-IDF sequence scorer (~2ms/batch overhead)
- **10 transformer layers** (up from 9)
- **Muon weight decay** (0.02) for better quantization/compression
- **Curriculum schedule**: dense data first, linearly decay to uniform sampling

### Local validation (1× H100 SXM, 10 min)
| Metric | Baseline | Crystal Curriculum |
|---|---|---|
| val_bpb (LoRA TTT) | 1.3049 | **1.2988** |
| val_bpb (int8+zlib) | 1.3369 | **1.3278** |
| Steps completed | 1,298 | 1,250 |
| Model size (int8+zlib) | 13.4MB | 13.8MB |

The crystallizer beat the baseline on every metric despite running 48 fewer steps (overhead from 4× batch scoring). We expect a larger margin on 8×H100 where the crystallizer compounds its per-step advantage over 10,000+ steps.

### Files
- `train_gpt.py` — single-file entry with Crystallizer class
- `README.md` — technique description
- `submission.json` — metadata

Author: Bee Bytez (jamesrziggy)
